summary: Improved tools/split_suggester.py, a helper that analyzes float constants in source ASM files to suggest file boundaries and generate C file templates. The PR body says this recreated #742 and specifically added map-file function names to the console preview, excluded duplicate floats from generated function headers, and changed generated include lines from a local quoted filename include to a project-relative angle-bracket include. The diff also shows a broad Python style cleanup while preserving the underlying split heuristic logic.

key_files.why_it_matters: Only changed file. It contains the split suggestion logic, map-name lookup, preview printing, and generated C template/header-comment output.

what_changed: Bumped the script comment version from 1.0 to 1.1 and updated the author line to Daniel R. Cappel.
Added console-preview support for map-file function names: when map names exist, the script prints a note and shows each internal function label with the resolved map name in parentheses.
Changed generated C template includes from `#include "{filename}.h"` to a computed project-relative include of the form `#include <.../{filename}.h>`.
Built the include path by walking `newParentDir` up to the `melee` folder and joining path parts with `/`.
Deduplicated float literal comment output per generated function by tracking labels in a `uniqueSamples` set before writing `// label (single/double: value)` lines.
Improved singular/plural console output for the suggested file count, printing `Suggesting 1 file` when there is exactly one split.
Applied a large Python formatting/style pass: normalized quotes, spacing, function-call formatting, class declaration style, import ordering, and multi-line formatting for long conditions and prints.

The PR text mentions 'function names for Sheik renamed', but the provided changed-files slice only contains `tools/split_suggester.py`; there is no direct diff evidence here for Sheik symbol/file changes.
smart_moves: The generated include path was made project-relative rather than source-file-local, matching decomp source include style better and avoiding fragile includes like `"filename.h"` when files live in nested character/source directories.
The preview now surfaces map-resolved function names before file generation, letting decompilers validate suggested splits against known symbols without opening the generated files.
Deduplicating function-header float comments keeps generated templates cleaner when the same literal label is referenced multiple times by one function.
The map-name display is gated on `func.mapName`, so existing output remains available even when no map file is supplied or no map name was resolved.
The PR kept the core float-boundary heuristic intact while improving the output quality and readability of generated scaffolding.
decomp_lessons: For split-helper tooling, console previews should include both raw labels and map-resolved names when available; this shortens review of proposed file boundaries.
Generated C templates should use the repository's normal include path convention, not just the local basename, because initial scaffolding often persists into real decomp source.
When generated comments summarize literals, deduplicate per function to avoid noisy headers and make the useful constants easier to scan.
If a tool computes paths from repository structure, make the assumption explicit in code; here the include path walk stops at a folder named `melee`, which future users should know is required.
Large formatting-only edits mixed with behavior changes make diffs harder to review; future PRs may be easier to audit if style cleanup and functional script changes are separated.
naming_conventions: Generated include path uses angle brackets with a repo-relative path, e.g. `#include <melee/.../{filename}.h>`, based on the code's walk up to the `melee` directory.
Map names are accepted only when they do not start with `zz` and do not end with `_`, preserving the script's filter against placeholder or undesirable map symbols.

For character source folders under `\ft\chara`, the script derives a character folder name as `ft` plus an uppercased third filename character plus the rest of the filename, e.g. via `charFolderName = "ft" + filename[2].upper() + filename[3:]`.
Generated file names retain the original split source basename and append a numeric suffix such as `{filename}1.c`; if not overwriting, existing outputs receive ` - NEW.c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The tool infers file boundaries by tracking `.sdata2` float labels referenced from `lfs` and `lfd` instructions in source ASM comments.
It treats a new instance of an already-used float literal value as a likely file split, unless already handled by padding-break logic.
It uses suspicious padding between literal entries and the next single-precision float as another boundary hint.
It scans branches around `blr` and labels to avoid treating every `blr` as a certain function end when a branch targets the following label.
It performs a second pass that moves earlier required functions into later suggested files when later functions reference helpers from the previous file.

review_feedback.takeaway: Approved without recorded review-comment feedback in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find PR #742 for the original split_suggester improvements and any Sheik rename diff not present in this PR slice.
Search later PRs for fixes to split_suggester include path generation on non-Windows paths, since this script still uses Windows-style `\asm\`, `\src\`, and `\ft\chara` path checks.
Search for later changes to duplicate float handling or generated function header format in `tools/split_suggester.py`.
